Based on the `re_exports` data from the graph, I have a complete picture. Two files re-export `OrbitError` via `pub use`:

1. `crates/orbit-common/src/types/mod.rs` — `{"name":"OrbitError","source_path":"error::OrbitError"}`

**Reasoning:**
- `crates/orbit-common/src/types/error.rs` — original `struct OrbitError` definition; excluded per the question.
- `crates/orbit-common/src/types/mod.rs` — re-exports `OrbitError` from `error::OrbitError` (flat re-export of the submodule's type).
- `crates/orbit-core/src/lib.rs` — re-exports `OrbitError` from `orbit_common::types::OrbitError` (cross-crate convenience re-export).
- All other crate `lib.rs` files were checked and contain no `OrbitError` entry in their `re_exports`.
